assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 159: 'Assertion specificity | -- | -1 (assertion relaxed: full PURL with qualifiers to versioned PURL without qualifiers in `test_recommend_purls_basic`)'. Lines 169-170: 'Assertion relaxation in `test_recommend_purls_basic`: the PURL assertion changed from checking the fully qualified string `"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar"` to checking only the versioned string `"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12"`. This is a semantic relaxation -- the assertion is less specific about the response content, covering fewer aspects of the PURL structure.'

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment

Detailed analysis section begins at line 123: '## Test Change Classification: Detailed Analysis' spanning lines 123-209, containing: Overview (lines 127-128), File-Level Classification table (lines 130-136), Structural Assessment for purl_recommend.rs (lines 138-170) with base/PR branch function comparison and signal tally table, Structural Assessment for purl_simplify.rs (lines 172-178), Semantic Assessment (lines 180-193), and Combined Classification (lines 195-209).

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 138-139: 'Comparison of base-branch and PR-branch versions:' followed by listing of base branch functions (lines 141-145) and PR branch functions (lines 147-151). The structural assessment references specific test file content: function names (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers, test_recommend_purls_dedup), assertion strings ('pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=...'), and assertion types (contains('?') negative assertions). The semantic assessment (lines 180-193) discusses coverage changes in terms of what the tests verify, not in terms of acceptance criteria or task requirements. The classification (lines 195-209) is derived from structural signal counts (functions added/removed, assertions added/relaxed).
